questions.text: Rectangular coordinate system is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Polar coordinate system uses a radial distance and an angle to represent points
Cylindrical coordinate system combines polar coordinates with a vertical axis.
Cartesian coordinate system, also known as rectangular coordinate system, uses two or three perpendicular axes to define points in space.
Spherical coordinate system uses radial distance, polar angle, and azimuthal angle.

questions.explanation: Cartesian coordinate system, also known as rectangular coordinate system, uses two or three perpendicular axes to define points in space.

questions.text: The direction of a vector in space is specified by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option (a) one angle is not sufficient to specify the direction in three-dimensional space
A vector in space is often specified using two angles in a three-dimensional coordinate system, typically denoted as polar and azimuthal angles.
Option (c) three angles are unnecessary; two angles are enough to define the orientation
Option (d) no angle is incorrect because angles are generally used to specify the direction of a vector in space.

questions.explanation: A vector in space is often specified using two angles in a three-dimensional coordinate system, typically denoted as polar and azimuthal angles.

questions.text: Addition of vectors obey:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Addition of vectors obeys the commutative law, meaning that changing the order of the vectors being added does not affect the result.It also obeys the associative law, meaning that the grouping of vectors in the addition operation does not affect the result.The distributive law is also applicable, by multiplying a vector by a scalar and then adding them.

questions.explanation: Addition of vectors obeys the commutative law, meaning that changing the order of the vectors being added does not affect the result.It also obeys the associative law, meaning that the grouping of vectors in the addition operation does not affect the result.The distributive law is also applicable, by multiplying a vector by a scalar and then adding them.

options.text: A and B must be parallel and in the same direction
the angle between A and B must be 90.

Therefore it is the correct option. They cant be parallel and in the same direction because that will create a result greater than expected.They cant be parallel and in the opposite direction because the answer will never be equal as there is a modulus on the LHS.

questions.text: For a body to be in equilibrium under the combined action of several forces:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: all the forces must be applied at the same point
options.explanation: Option (a) is not necessary; forces can be applied at different points as long as they contribute to a net force of zero.

options.text: all of the forces form pairs of equal and opposite forces
options.explanation: Option (b) is a condition for equilibrium, but it is not the only condition

options.text: two of these forces must be balanced by a third force
options.explanation: Option (c) is not a general requirement for equilibrium; it's possible for multiple forces to contribute to equilibrium without a specific force balancing two others.

options.text: the sum of the components of all the forces in any direction must equal zero
options.explanation: For a body to be in equilibrium, the net force acting on it must be zero.This implies that the sum of the forces in any direction must be zero.

questions.explanation: For a body to be in equilibrium, the net force acting on it must be zero.This implies that the sum of the forces in any direction must be zero.

questions.text: Torque acting on a body determines:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Options (a) and (b) refer to linear quantities (acceleration and linear acceleration), which are associated with translational motion, not rotational motion.
Options (a) and (b) refer to linear quantities (acceleration and linear acceleration), which are associated with translational motion, not rotational motion.

Torque is the rotational equivalent of force and is defined as the product of force and the lever arm (distance from the axis of rotation).Torque causes angular acceleration, which is the rate of change of angular velocity. It is responsible for changes in the rotational motion of a body. Therefore, the correct answer is (c) angular acceleration.

options.text: direction of motion of the body
options.explanation: Option (d) is not directly determined by torque; torque is more directly related to the rotational motion itself.

questions.explanation: Torque is the rotational equivalent of force and is defined as the product of force and the lever arm (distance from the axis of rotation).Torque causes angular acceleration, which is the rate of change of angular velocity. It is responsible for changes in the rotational motion of a body. Therefore, the correct answer is (c) angular acceleration.

questions.explanation: Torque = force x displacement (Nm)So, [Torque] = [force][displacement][Torque] = [MLT-2][M][Torque] = [ML2T-2]

questions.text: A body will be in complete equilibrium when it is satisfying:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The first condition of equilibrium states that the vector sum of all the forces acting on a body must be zero for the body to be in translational equilibrium.The second condition of equilibrium states that the sum of the torques (or moments) acting on a body about any axis must be zero for the body to be in rotational equilibrium.For a body to be in complete equilibrium, both the first and second conditions must be satisfied.Therefore, the correct answer is (c) both 1st and 2nd condition of equilibrium.

questions.explanation: The first condition of equilibrium states that the vector sum of all the forces acting on a body must be zero for the body to be in translational equilibrium.The second condition of equilibrium states that the sum of the torques (or moments) acting on a body about any axis must be zero for the body to be in rotational equilibrium.For a body to be in complete equilibrium, both the first and second conditions must be satisfied.Therefore, the correct answer is (c) both 1st and 2nd condition of equilibrium.

questions.text: Three identical uniform rods are each acted upon by two or more forces, all perpendicular to the rods and all equal in magnitude. Which of the rods could be in static equilibrium if an additional force is applied at the center of mass of the rod?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: For rod 1, if we apply force downward, all of the forces will be balanced and hence, it is in static equilibrium
If we apply force for rod 2, it will move up if applied force is up and vice versa
If we apply force on rod 3, it will move upwards because net force is upwards

questions.explanation: For rod 1, if we apply force downward, all of the forces will be balanced and hence, it is in static equilibrium

questions.text: Three coplanar forces acting on a body keep it in equilibrium. They should therefore be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: For a body to be in equilibrium, the net force acting on it must be zero, and the net torque (or moment) about any point must be zero.If three coplanar forces are keeping a body in equilibrium, they must be concurrent, meaning they all meet at a single point (the point where the net torque is calculated).Therefore, the correct answer is (a) concurrent.

questions.explanation: For a body to be in equilibrium, the net force acting on it must be zero, and the net torque (or moment) about any point must be zero.If three coplanar forces are keeping a body in equilibrium, they must be concurrent, meaning they all meet at a single point (the point where the net torque is calculated).Therefore, the correct answer is (a) concurrent.

questions.text: A central force is that which:﻿
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option a is incorrect. A central force, by definition, acts along the radial direction, and as such, it does not produce torque. Torque is produced when a force is applied at a distance from the axis of rotation.

Option b is the correct option. A central force acts along the line joining the particle to the center of force. Since it is directed radially, there is no lever arm (perpendicular distance) between the point of application and the axis of rotation, resulting in no torque.

options.text: sometime can produce torque some time can't
options.explanation: Option c is also incorrect. The nature of a central force is such that it always acts along the radial direction, and it never produces torque.

Option d is incorrect as well. While a central force itself doesn't produce torque, understanding the nature of central forces can be crucial in analyzing the dynamics of objects undergoing circular motion, where centripetal forces come into play.

questions.explanation: Option b is the correct option. A central force acts along the line joining the particle to the center of force. Since it is directed radially, there is no lever arm (perpendicular distance) between the point of application and the axis of rotation, resulting in no torque.

two forces act on the wheel

Two hands do provide firm grip but that doesn’t make it easy to turn.
Couple means two equal and opposite forces on the same body at different parts. So, Couple makes it easy to turn the car as twice the force is applied using two hands.

questions.explanation: Couple means two equal and opposite forces on the same body at different parts. So, Couple makes it easy to turn the car as twice the force is applied using two hands.

questions.text: The vector quantity in the following is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is not a vector quantity because it represents the length of path travelled by an object and only has magnitude. It has no direction.

Vector quantity is a quantity that has both magnitude and direction. Impulse is a vector quantity because it has both magnitude and direction. It is defined as the product of both force and time. The direction of the force applied determines the direction of the impulse.
Energy is a scalar quantity and not a vector quantity because it represents the capacity to do work or transfer heat. No direction is represented as it only gives the magnitude.
It is considered a scalar quantity because it is a pure numerical value with no associated direction. Scalars can be positive or negative, but they have no specific orientation.

questions.explanation: A vector quantity is a quantity that has both magnitude and direction. Impulse is a vector quantity because it has both magnitude and direction. It is defined as the product of both force and time. The direction of the force applied determines the direction of the impulse.

questions.text: The minimum number of unequal vectors whose vector sum can be zero are
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The minimum number of unequal vectors whose vector sum can be zero is 3. This is because three non-collinear vectors can form a triangle, where the vector sum of the sides can result in a closed loop, hence yielding a resultant vector of zero magnitude.
Two vectors can only have a resultant of zero if they are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, but the question asks for "unequal vectors."
Four non-coplanar vectors in 3D space can result in a zero vector sum, but the question asks for the minimum number.
Five vectors are not necessary to achieve a zero vector sum. Three vectors are the minimum required.

questions.explanation: The minimum number of unequal vectors whose vector sum can be zero is 3. This is because three non-collinear vectors can form a triangle, where the vector sum of the sides can result in a closed loop, hence yielding a resultant vector of zero magnitude.

The dot product of r and L, denoted as r⋅L, is zero. This is because the cross product L=r×P results in a vector perpendicular to both r and P, thus making the dot product zero.
This expression does not consider the angle between r and L and is not the dot product of the two vectors.
This expression resembles the magnitude of the cross product of r and L, but the question asks for the dot product, which yields a scalar, not a vector magnitude.

questions.explanation: The dot product of r and L, denoted as r⋅L, is zero. This is because the cross product L=r×P results in a vector perpendicular to both r and P, thus making the dot product zero.

Vector resolution refers to the process of breaking down a vector into its components, not the reverse of vector addition.

The reverse process of vector addition is subtraction of vectors. When you subtract one vector from another, you are essentially reversing the addition process by adding the negative of one vector to the other.
Dot product involves multiplication of vectors but is not the reverse process of vector addition.

questions.text: Three vectors A, B, and C satisfy the relation A⋅B=0 and A⋅C=0. Then A is parallel to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the cross product of B and C, not necessarily parallel to A.

Since A⋅B=0, it implies that vectors A and B are orthogonal (perpendicular) to each other. Similarly, A⋅C=0, suggesting that A and C are also orthogonal. Therefore, A must be parallel to the vector B.

questions.text: Sum of magnitudes of two forces acting at a point is 16N, if their resultant is normal to the smaller force and has a magnitude of 8N, and then the forces are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Here's how to solve this problem and why option (a) is the correct answer:Relate the forces to the resultant:Let's denote the two forces as F1 and F2, and the resultant force as R.Since the resultant is perpendicular (normal) to the smaller force, we know that the angle between them is 90 degrees.We can use the Pythagorean theorem to relate the magnitudes of the forces:R^2 = F1^2 + F2^2Incorporate the given information:We are given that the sum of the magnitudes of the two forces (F1 + F2) is 16 N.We are also given that the magnitude of the resultant (R) is 8 N.Solve for the forces:We can rewrite the equation from step 1 as: F2^2 = R^2 - F1^2 = 8^2 - F1^2Substitute the given information about the sum of the forces: F1 + F2 = 16 N --> F2 = 16 N - F1Substitute this expression for F2 in the previous equation: (16 N - F1)^2 = 8^2 - F1^2Expand and solve for F1: 256 N^2 - 32 F1 N + F1^2 = 64 N^2 - F1^2Combine like terms and rearrange: 2 F1^2 - 32 F1 N + 192 N^2 = 0Factor the expression: 2(F1 - 6 N)(F1 - 16 N) = 0Therefore, F1 = 6 N or F1 = 16 N.Find the corresponding value of the other force:Since we know F1 can be either 6 N or 16 N, we can find the corresponding value of F2 using the equation F1 + F2 = 16 N.If F1 = 6 N, then F2 = 16 N - 6 N = 10 N.If F1 = 16 N, then F2 = 16 N - 16 N = 0 N (which is not physically possible in this scenario).Therefore, the correct answer is a. 6N, 10N
This combination of forces would result in a resultant with a magnitude of 11.3 N (using the Pythagorean theorem), which is not equal to the given value of 8 N
Similar to option (b), this combination would also result in a resultant magnitude that does not match the given value
This combination is eliminated for the same reason as options (b) and (c). It would not produce a resultant magnitude of 8 N

questions.explanation: Here's how to solve this problem and why option (a) is the correct answer:Relate the forces to the resultant:Let's denote the two forces as F1 and F2, and the resultant force as R.Since the resultant is perpendicular (normal) to the smaller force, we know that the angle between them is 90 degrees.We can use the Pythagorean theorem to relate the magnitudes of the forces:R^2 = F1^2 + F2^2Incorporate the given information:We are given that the sum of the magnitudes of the two forces (F1 + F2) is 16 N.We are also given that the magnitude of the resultant (R) is 8 N.Solve for the forces:We can rewrite the equation from step 1 as: F2^2 = R^2 - F1^2 = 8^2 - F1^2Substitute the given information about the sum of the forces: F1 + F2 = 16 N --> F2 = 16 N - F1Substitute this expression for F2 in the previous equation: (16 N - F1)^2 = 8^2 - F1^2Expand and solve for F1: 256 N^2 - 32 F1 N + F1^2 = 64 N^2 - F1^2Combine like terms and rearrange: 2 F1^2 - 32 F1 N + 192 N^2 = 0Factor the expression: 2(F1 - 6 N)(F1 - 16 N) = 0Therefore, F1 = 6 N or F1 = 16 N.Find the corresponding value of the other force:Since we know F1 can be either 6 N or 16 N, we can find the corresponding value of F2 using the equation F1 + F2 = 16 N.If F1 = 6 N, then F2 = 16 N - 6 N = 10 N.If F1 = 16 N, then F2 = 16 N - 16 N = 0 N (which is not physically possible in this scenario).Therefore, the correct answer is a. 6N, 10N

questions.text: Two bodies of mass 4kg and 5 kg are moving along east and month directions with velocities 5 m/s and 3m/s respectively .magnitude of the velocity of center of mass of the systems is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Here's how to find the magnitude of the velocity of the center of mass of the system:Calculate the total momentum:Momentum of the 4 kg mass (p1): 4 kg * 5 m/s (east) = 20 kg m/s (east)Momentum of the 5 kg mass (p2): 5 kg * 3 m/s (north) = 15 kg m/s (north)Total momentum (p_total): p1 + p2 = 20 kg m/s (east) + 15 kg m/s (north)Calculate the total mass:Total mass (m_total): 4 kg + 5 kg = 9 kgFind the velocity of the center of mass (v_cm):v_cm = p_total / m_totalv_cm = (20 kg m/s (east) + 15 kg m/s (north)) / 9 kgv_cm = (20/9) m/s (east) + (15/9) m/s (north)Calculate the magnitude of v_cm:To find the magnitude of a vector (v), we use the Pythagorean theorem:|v| = √(v_x^2 + v_y^2)In this case:v_x = 20/9 m/s (east)v_y = 15/9 m/s (north)Therefore:|v_cm| = √((20/9)^2 + (15/9)^2)|v_cm| ≈ 25/9 m/sTherefore, the magnitude of the velocity of the centre of mass of the system is 25/9 m/s, which corresponds to answer choice a.

questions.explanation: Here's how to find the magnitude of the velocity of the center of mass of the system:Calculate the total momentum:Momentum of the 4 kg mass (p1): 4 kg * 5 m/s (east) = 20 kg m/s (east)Momentum of the 5 kg mass (p2): 5 kg * 3 m/s (north) = 15 kg m/s (north)Total momentum (p_total): p1 + p2 = 20 kg m/s (east) + 15 kg m/s (north)Calculate the total mass:Total mass (m_total): 4 kg + 5 kg = 9 kgFind the velocity of the center of mass (v_cm):v_cm = p_total / m_totalv_cm = (20 kg m/s (east) + 15 kg m/s (north)) / 9 kgv_cm = (20/9) m/s (east) + (15/9) m/s (north)Calculate the magnitude of v_cm:To find the magnitude of a vector (v), we use the Pythagorean theorem:|v| = √(v_x^2 + v_y^2)In this case:v_x = 20/9 m/s (east)v_y = 15/9 m/s (north)Therefore:|v_cm| = √((20/9)^2 + (15/9)^2)|v_cm| ≈ 25/9 m/sTherefore, the magnitude of the velocity of the centre of mass of the system is 25/9 m/s, which corresponds to answer choice a.

questions.text: The magnitude of the resultant of two forces Is 2F. if the magnitude of each force Is F, then the angle between these forces is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This implies the forces are acting in the same direction, resulting in a combined magnitude of 2F directly. However, this wouldn't be the case if the resultant has the same magnitude (F) as each individual force
When two forces are perpendicular (90° angle), their components add in quadrature (using the Pythagorean theorem). This means the resultant magnitude would be √(F^2 + F^2) = √2F, not 2F

When two forces have the same magnitude (F) and act at an angle of 120°, their components partially cancel each other out, but not completely. Using vector addition principles and the cosine rule, the resultant magnitude can be calculated as:Resultant magnitude = √(F^2 + F^2 - 2 * F * F * cos(120°))= √(F^2 + F^2 + F^2) = √(3F^2)= F√3This matches the given condition of the resultant having the same magnitude (F) as each individual force.

If the forces are acting in opposite directions (180° angle), their components completely cancel each other out, resulting in a zero resultant magnitude, not 2F. Therefore, based on the information about the resultant magnitude being the same (F) as each individual force (F), the angle between the two forces must be 120°

questions.explanation: When two forces have the same magnitude (F) and act at an angle of 120°, their components partially cancel each other out, but not completely. Using vector addition principles and the cosine rule, the resultant magnitude can be calculated as:Resultant magnitude = √(F^2 + F^2 - 2 * F * F * cos(120°))= √(F^2 + F^2 + F^2) = √(3F^2)= F√3This matches the given condition of the resultant having the same magnitude (F) as each individual force.

questions.text: The magnitude of the vector A,B and C are respectively 12,5 and 13 units and vector A+B=Cthen the angle between vector A and B is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Here's how we can find the angle between vectors A and B:Recognize the relationship: We are given that A + B = C, which means vector C is the resultant of adding vectors A and B head-to-tail.Apply the Law of Cosines: The Law of Cosines relates the sides and angles of a triangle. We can use it to find the angle between A and B, considering triangle ABC formed by vectors A, B, and C.Steps:Identify the sides:A = 12 (magnitude of vector A)B = 5 (magnitude of vector B)C = 13 (magnitude of vector C, given)Apply the Law of Cosines:cos(θ) = (A^2 + B^2 - C^2) / (2 * A * B)where θ is the angle between vectors A and B.Plug in the values:cos(θ) = (12^2 + 5^2 - 13^2) / (2 * 12 * 5)= -3 / 60= -1 / 20Find the angle:θ = arccos(-1 / 20) ≈ 1.5708 radiansSince the question asks for the angle in terms of π, convert the answer to degrees:θ ≈ 1.5708 radians * (180° / π) ≈ 90°However, the question asks for the smallest positive angle. Looking at the options and considering the negative cosine value, the correct answer is the angle whose cosine is -1/20 but lies in Quadrant I, which is:θ ≈ π/4 ≈ 45°Therefore, the angle between vectors A and B is π/4 (approximately 45 degrees).

questions.explanation: Here's how we can find the angle between vectors A and B:Recognize the relationship: We are given that A + B = C, which means vector C is the resultant of adding vectors A and B head-to-tail.Apply the Law of Cosines: The Law of Cosines relates the sides and angles of a triangle. We can use it to find the angle between A and B, considering triangle ABC formed by vectors A, B, and C.Steps:Identify the sides:A = 12 (magnitude of vector A)B = 5 (magnitude of vector B)C = 13 (magnitude of vector C, given)Apply the Law of Cosines:cos(θ) = (A^2 + B^2 - C^2) / (2 * A * B)where θ is the angle between vectors A and B.Plug in the values:cos(θ) = (12^2 + 5^2 - 13^2) / (2 * 12 * 5)= -3 / 60= -1 / 20Find the angle:θ = arccos(-1 / 20) ≈ 1.5708 radiansSince the question asks for the angle in terms of π, convert the answer to degrees:θ ≈ 1.5708 radians * (180° / π) ≈ 90°However, the question asks for the smallest positive angle. Looking at the options and considering the negative cosine value, the correct answer is the angle whose cosine is -1/20 but lies in Quadrant I, which is:θ ≈ π/4 ≈ 45°Therefore, the angle between vectors A and B is π/4 (approximately 45 degrees).

questions.text: three vector satisfy the relation vector A .B=0 and A.C=0 then A is parallel to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Conditions for two vectors to be parallel:Two vectors are considered parallel if they have the same direction or point in opposite directions. Mathematically, this can be expressed as:Zero dot product: The dot product of two parallel vectors is zero. This is because the dot product represents the projection of one vector onto another, and if they are parallel, the projection will be zero.Scalar multiple of one another: One vector can be obtained by multiplying the other vector by a scalar (a non-zero number). This means they have the same direction, just scaled by a factor.Analysis of the given vectors:We are given that the dot product of A with both B and C is zero:A.B = 0A.C = 0Based on the condition for parallel vectors, we cannot definitively say that A is parallel to either B or C individually. The dot product being zero only indicates that A is orthogonal (perpendicular) to both B and C.However, the fact that A is orthogonal to both B and C suggests an interesting relationship:A is parallel to the cross product of B and C (B x C):The cross product of two vectors B and C results in a vector that is orthogonal to both B and C. This is because the cross product essentially creates a vector that is perpendicular to the plane formed by B and C.Therefore, based on the given conditions, A is parallel to the cross product of B and C (B x C)

questions.explanation: Conditions for two vectors to be parallel:Two vectors are considered parallel if they have the same direction or point in opposite directions. Mathematically, this can be expressed as:Zero dot product: The dot product of two parallel vectors is zero. This is because the dot product represents the projection of one vector onto another, and if they are parallel, the projection will be zero.Scalar multiple of one another: One vector can be obtained by multiplying the other vector by a scalar (a non-zero number). This means they have the same direction, just scaled by a factor.Analysis of the given vectors:We are given that the dot product of A with both B and C is zero:A.B = 0A.C = 0Based on the condition for parallel vectors, we cannot definitively say that A is parallel to either B or C individually. The dot product being zero only indicates that A is orthogonal (perpendicular) to both B and C.However, the fact that A is orthogonal to both B and C suggests an interesting relationship:A is parallel to the cross product of B and C (B x C):The cross product of two vectors B and C results in a vector that is orthogonal to both B and C. This is because the cross product essentially creates a vector that is perpendicular to the plane formed by B and C.Therefore, based on the given conditions, A is parallel to the cross product of B and C (B x C)

questions.text: the dot product of force and velocity is equal to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Force (F): A force is a vector quantity with magnitude and direction.Velocity (v): Velocity is another vector quantity with magnitude and direction.Power (P): Power is the rate at which work is done, defined as the work done per unit time.When we take the dot product of force (F) and velocity (v), we essentially multiply the magnitude of the force component in the direction of the velocity with the magnitude of the velocity itself. This product reflects the rate of work being done by the force on the object, which is precisely the definition of power.Therefore, the dot product of force and velocity is equal to power

questions.explanation: The right-hand rule is a mnemonic for the cross product of two vectors in three-dimensional space. If you have two vectors, A and B, and you want to find their cross-product A×B, you can use the right-hand rule as follows:Point your index finger in the direction of vector A.Point your middle finger in the direction of vector B.Your thumb then points in the direction of A×B, the cross product.

questions.text: The dot product of two vectors is negative i

options.explanation: Incorrect. The dot product is positive for parallel vectors
Correct. The dot product of perpendicular vectors is zeroIn summary, the correct option is **b. They are perpendicular vectors** for the dot product to be negative
Incorrect. The dot product is negative for anti-parallel vectors
Incorrect. Vectors themselves don't have a sign; the dot product is negative for specific relative orientations

questions.text: The magnitude of the resultant of two forces is 2F. if the magnitude of each force is F, then the angle b/w these forces is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the angle between the forces were 0°, their resultant magnitude would be \(2F\). However, in this scenario, the given resultant magnitude is \(2F\), indicating that the forces are not aligned and must have an angle between them.

When the magnitude of the resultant of two forces is \(2F\) and the magnitude of each force is \(F\), the maximum resultant magnitude occurs when the forces are perpendicular to each other, resulting in an angle of \(90°\) between them.
This option suggests an angle that is greater than 90°, which would result in a larger resultant magnitude than \(2F\). It is not consistent with the given information.

If the angle between the forces were 180°, they would be acting in opposite directions, canceling each other out completely, resulting in a resultant magnitude of 0. However, the given resultant magnitude is \(2F\), indicating that the angle between the forces cannot be 180°.

questions.explanation: When the magnitude of the resultant of two forces is \(2F\) and the magnitude of each force is \(F\), the maximum resultant magnitude occurs when the forces are perpendicular to each other, resulting in an angle of \(90°\) between them.

questions.text: \(I(kxj)\) is equal to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The dot product of two orthogonal vectors, such as \(k\) and \(j\), results in 0, not -1.

The cross product \(k \times j\) results in a vector perpendicular to both \(k\) and \(j\), which is a unit vector in the \(i\) direction. When this is multiplied by \(I\), which is also in the \(i\) direction, the result is 0.
The dot product of \(I\) with a unit vector in the \(i\) direction is 1, not 0.
There is no basis for this value in the given scenario, as the dot product of \(I\) with \(k \times j\) results in 0, not 2.

questions.explanation: The cross product \(k \times j\) results in a vector perpendicular to both \(k\) and \(j\), which is a unit vector in the \(i\) direction. When this is multiplied by \(I\), which is also in the \(i\) direction, the result is 0.

questions.text: Three vectors of equal magnitude are acting on the three sides of an equilateral triangle. The magnitude of their resultant is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the vectors were arranged in such a way that they canceled each other out completely, the resultant would be 0. However, in an equilateral triangle arrangement, the resultant is non-zero.
This option suggests a value that is greater than the magnitude of each individual vector, which is not the case for the resultant of vectors in an equilateral triangle.
When three vectors of equal magnitude are acting on the three sides of an equilateral triangle, the magnitude of their resultant is equal to the length of any side multiplied by the square root of 3.
This value does not correspond to the magnitude of the resultant of vectors in an equilateral triangle arrangement. It is neither the sum nor the average of the individual magnitudes.

questions.explanation: When three vectors of equal magnitude are acting on the three sides of an equilateral triangle, the magnitude of their resultant is equal to the length of any side multiplied by the square root of 3.

questions.text: The dot product of force and velocity is equal to
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The dot product of force and velocity gives the power, which is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred.
The dot product of force and velocity does not give impulse, which is the change in momentum of an object.
A couple is a pair of forces of equal magnitude acting in parallel but opposite directions, resulting in rotational motion, not linear motion.
The dot product of force and velocity does not give momentum, which is the product of an object's mass and velocity.

questions.explanation: The dot product of force and velocity gives the power, which is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred.

questions.text: If a force of 2N acting through a moment arm of 2 m produces a torque of 6 Nm, what is the angle between F and r.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Substituting the angle 0 degree into equation 2 yields a torque, 0 Nm that conflicts with the given value of the torque as 6 Nm.
Substituting the angle 30 degrees into equation 2 yields a torque, 2 Nm that is wrong.
Substituting the angle 45 degrees into equation 2 yields a torque, 2 Nm that is wrong.

As we have the formula of torque asτ=r×FMagnitude: τ=rFsin -----(2)Given: τ=6 Nm, F=2 N, r=2 mTo Find: Using equation 2, Re arranging: θ=sin-1(rF)Substituting the values, θ=sin-1(622) = sin-1(32)θ=60°

questions.text: The cross product of three unit vectors (i, j, k) equals:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Is not possibly the answers because the cross product of three perpendicular vectors yields a null vector.
Is not possibly the answers because the cross product of three perpendicular vectors yields a null vector.

These vectors represent the direction of x, y, and z axes in space. Since these axes are mutually perpendicular, the given vectors have angles 90 degrees between them. Applying the concept of cross product of two perpendicular vectors (starting from left to right), i j = kand k k = 0i j k = 0 (In this question, only the magnitude is taken. Prefer the vector 0 if present)i.e., a null vector with a magnitude 0.
The value of cross product of two defined vectors can never be infinite because it is controlled by the factor sin that can never be infinite.

questions.text: If velocity v being a vector is multiplied to scalar quantity mass m, the result will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The product of a vector with a scalar or a dimensionless less quantity yields another vector parallel to the vector before the product. For example, the momentum of a body is a vector, a product of mass (scalar) with velocity (vector).
